summary: Matched the lbcardgame object by completing its data representation, making small codegen-sensitive C changes, correcting a struct offset comment, updating GALE01 symbols, and flipping src/melee/lb/lbcardgame.c from NonMatching to Matching in configure.py. The decomp.dev bot reported 4 new matches: lbcardgame .data, lb_8001CC84, lb_8001C658, and lb_8001C8BC all reaching 100%.
what_changed: lb_803BAB74 in lbcardgame.c was expanded from a zero placeholder to a full static initializer with header values {0, 3, 0, 0x1790, 0}, NULL x14, seven {0x1F2C, 1, NULL} entries, and a {-1, 0, NULL} sentinel.
The anonymous array element fields in lb_803BAB74 changed from UNK_T x0/x4 to u32 x0/x4, matching the numeric initializer usage.
lb_8001C658 changed time conversion from manual OSGetTime() / OS_TIMER_CLOCK to OSTicksToSeconds(temp_r6), then OSSecondsToTicks((u64) seconds) before OSTicksToCalendarTime.
lb_8001CC84 removed an unused local and changed the reset to lb_80433318.x10 = temp_r3 = 0, a codegen-sensitive chained assignment.
lbcardgame.static.h corrected the documented offset for lb_80433318_t._1C from /* +30 */ to /* +1C */.

config/GALE01/symbols.txt updated lbcardgame-adjacent data boundaries and labels, including changing lb_803BAB74 size from 0x28 to 0x78 and replacing several guessed global/local names with compiler-style @number labels.
smart_moves: Matched .data by modeling the emitted blob as one structured initializer instead of leaving lb_803BAB74 as a zero todo placeholder.
Used concrete u32 field types for numeric data fields rather than preserving opaque UNK_T types, improving both layout clarity and initializer/codegen behavior.
Replaced an algebraically equivalent manual timer division with the SDK-style OSTicksToSeconds/OSSecondsToTicks sequence, which produced the needed match for lb_8001C658.
Used a chained assignment in lb_8001CC84 to force the local temp_r3 and the global state field to be cleared together, preserving expected compiler behavior.
Cleaned symbol ownership around the matched data by using anonymous @number labels for strings/constants that did not have evidence for meaningful lbArchive/lb names.
decomp_lessons: For data matching, verify whether adjacent unnamed words/strings are actually part of a larger struct object; symbol size changes can be required alongside C initializer changes.
Do not keep UNK_T when the initializer and accesses show a normal 32-bit integer field; concrete types can be necessary for matching and make later field naming safer.
SDK helper calls can matter even when arithmetic is equivalent: OS tick/time conversions should prefer the original library idiom when matching.
Small source-expression choices such as assignment chaining can be legitimate matching tactics for register/local lifetime differences.
Static header offset comments are part of decomp maintainability; fix misleading offsets when touching the struct.
A configure.py NonMatching-to-Matching flip should be paired with symbols/data cleanup and bot-confirmed 100% matches.
naming_conventions: Use @number labels in symbols.txt for anonymous compiler-generated constants/strings when there is no evidence for a semantic global name.
Keep the real data object name lb_803BAB74 for the lbcardgame static struct and update its symbol size to cover the full emitted object.
Preserve static header offset-comment style with byte offsets matching the field, e.g. /* +1C */ char _1C[0x40];.

The PR provides evidence against some guessed lbArchive_/lb_ names in this address range, replacing them with @12, @13, @23, @62, @126, @162, @163, @164, @173, @203, @204, @334, @335, @336, @341, @342, @343, and @344.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Static data tactic: expand lb_803BAB74 into an exact initializer and adjust symbols.txt from a 0x28 object plus adjacent data to one 0x78 object.
Time conversion tactic: use u32 seconds = OSTicksToSeconds(OSGetTime()) and cast back with OSSecondsToTicks((u64) seconds) before OSTicksToCalendarTime.
Register/lifetime tactic: assign lb_80433318.x10 = temp_r3 = 0 instead of only storing zero to the struct field.
Symbol tactic: rename anonymous data/string/float constants to @number labels to align with compiler-local symbol expectations.
Build tactic: only after the source and symbol tweaks, mark melee/lb/lbcardgame.c as Matching in configure.py.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the slice, and the PR body was empty, so reviewer intent is not evidenced.

The decomp.dev bot reported GALE01 improvements: matched code 61.14% (+0.03%, +1328 bytes), linked code 33.68% (+0.08%, +3260 bytes), matched data 37.80% (+0.03%, +360 bytes), and linked data 32.32% (+0.04%, +504 bytes).
The bot listed 4 new matches for main/melee/lb/lbcardgame: .data, lb_8001CC84, lb_8001C658, and lb_8001C8BC.
follow_up_queries: Search for other manual OSGetTime() / OS_TIMER_CLOCK conversions that may need OSTicksToSeconds for matching.
Inspect lb_8001C8BC if researching why it became a one-byte new match despite no direct source hunk in this slice.
Use lb_803BAB74's completed initializer as the reference point before renaming its anonymous fields.
Audit nearby GALE01 symbol labels if future lbArchive/lbDvd/lbSnap work depends on the anonymous @number constants changed here.
